name: Time — Reading Clocks and Counting Minutes
whatIsIt: A clock is a circular number line! The SHORT hand counts hours; the LONG hand counts minutes — 5 minutes for every number it passes. 'Half past 3' means the minute hand travelled HALF the circle (30 minutes) — your fractions live on the clock face! Elapsed time is how long something took, from start to finish.
whyNeeded: School bells, cartoon timings, cricket overs, train departures — the day runs on clocks. Reading them (and counting how long until / how long it took) puts YOU in charge of your day instead of always asking someone.
realLifeUses: School: classes start at 9:00 and lunch at 12:30
TV: your cartoon starts at 5:30 and ends at 6:00 — how long is it?
Cooking: the cake needs 45 minutes in the oven
Travel: the bus leaves at 4:15 — how long do you have to pack?

vocabulary.meaning: The SHORT hand — moves once around in 12 hours.
The LONG hand — each clock number it passes = 5 minutes.
30 minutes past / 15 minutes past — clock fractions!
How much time passed between start and finish.

story.title: Half Past Three by the Wall Clock
story.text: Ravi reads the wall clock. 'The short hand is between 3 and 4, and the long hand points at 6 — what time is it, Thatha?' 'The minute hand has swept HALF the circle — that is 30 minutes. So it is half past three, 3:30.' Ravi nods: 'Short hand tells the hour, long hand tells the minutes.'
story.extractedProblem: The short hand is between 3 and 4; the long hand points at 6. What time is it?
story.answerInStory: 3:30 — half past three. The minute hand covered half the circle: 30 minutes.

visual.caption: The long hand walks the circle: to 3 = quarter (15 min), to 6 = half (30 min). Skip-count numbers by 5!

teachingGallery.title: Reading the clock
teachingGallery.note: The short hand = hours; the long hand = minutes (count by 5s).

examples.caption: Long hand on 6 → half past (30 min).

standardMethod.summary: Short hand → hour. Long hand → skip-count each number by 5 for minutes.
steps: Read the SHORT hand first: the hour is the number it last passed.
Read the LONG hand: skip-count by 5 for each number from 12 (1→5, 2→10, 3→15... 6→30).
Say it together: short hand past 3, long hand at 6 → 3:30.
Landmarks: 12 = o'clock, 3 = quarter past, 6 = half past.

alternateMethods.name: Counting-up elapsed time
alternateMethods.whenToUse: How long did it take? How long until?
steps: Start at the earlier time, climb to a friendly o'clock, then jump on.
Longer gaps: 4:15 → 5:00 is 45 min, 5:00 → 6:00 is 1 hour → total 1 h 45 min.
alternateMethods.example: School 9:00 to lunch 12:30 → 9→12 is 3 hours, +30 min = 3 h 30 min.

alternateMethods.name: Fraction-circle method
alternateMethods.whenToUse: To connect the clock to your fraction skills.
steps: The clock face is 1 whole hour = 60 minutes.
Long hand at 6 = 1/2 circle = 30 min; at 3 = 1/4 circle = 15 min; at 9 = 3/4 circle = 45 min.

workedExamples.problem: The short hand is just past 7; the long hand points at 3. What time is it?
Anu's cartoon runs 5:30 to 6:00. Ravi says it lasted '70 minutes because 6:00 − 5:30 = 0.70'. Untangle him!
steps: Clocks don't work in hundredths — an hour has 60 minutes, not 100!
That climb is 30 minutes. The cartoon lasted 30 minutes.
workedExamples.answer: 30 minutes — count up, and remember: 60 minutes per hour!

trickPractice.intro: Each clock number is 5 minutes — multiply the minute hand's number by 5.

questions.q: The long (minute) hand points to 4. How many minutes past the hour?
The long (minute) hand points to 3. How many minutes past the hour?
The long (minute) hand points to 6. How many minutes past the hour?
The long (minute) hand points to 9. How many minutes past the hour?
The long (minute) hand points to 2. How many minutes past the hour?
The long (minute) hand points to 7. How many minutes past the hour?

commonMistakes.mistake: Reading the long hand as the hour (calling 3:30 'six o'clock something').
commonMistakes.fix: SHORT hand = hour (it moves slowly, like hours do). LONG hand = minutes. Check the short hand FIRST, every time.

commonMistakes.mistake: Reading the long hand at 6 as '6 minutes' instead of 30.
commonMistakes.fix: The clock numbers are HOUR labels. For minutes, each number = 5: skip-count 5, 10, 15... The 6 is the 6th stop → 30 minutes.

commonMistakes.mistake: Treating an hour as 100 minutes when finding elapsed time (6:00 − 5:30 = '70').
commonMistakes.fix: One hour = 60 minutes! Never column-subtract clock times like money. COUNT UP instead: 5:30 → 6:00 is 30 minutes, always.

easy.q: Short hand between 4 and 5; long hand on the 6. Write the time like 4:30.
hintLadder: Short hand last passed which number?
Long hand at 6 = half the circle = how many minutes?

easy.q: The long hand points at the 3. How many MINUTES past the hour is that?

medium.q: Short hand just past 7, long hand on the 9. Write the time like 7:45.
hintLadder: Long hand at 9: skip-count by 5 nine times.

medium.q: Your cartoon runs from 5:30 to 6:00. How many minutes long is it?
hintLadder: 5:30 → 6:00 is how far around the circle?

challenge.q: Ravi computed 6:00 − 5:30 = '0:70, so 70 minutes'. What rule did he break?

options.label: An hour has 60 minutes — clock times can't be subtracted like money

challenge.answer: An hour has 60 minutes — clock times can't be subtracted like money
hintLadder: How many minutes in one hour?

challenge.q: The school bus leaves at 4:15. It's 3:30 now. How many minutes do you have?

questions.q: Short hand between 9 and 10, long hand on 6. Write the time like 9:30.
The long hand pointing at 6 means…
A movie starts at 2:00 and ends at 3:30. How many MINUTES did it run?

hintLadder: ...so the slow, short hand carries them.

teachBackPrompt: Teach the talking clock! Show someone at home a real clock at any time, read it aloud (short hand first!), and explain why 'half past' is your fraction 1/2 wearing a wristwatch.

revisionCard.summary: Short hand = hour; long hand = minutes (each number × 5). Landmarks: 3→quarter past (15), 6→half past (30), 9→quarter to (45). One hour = 60 minutes — NEVER subtract clocks like money; COUNT UP for elapsed time (5:30→6:00 = 30 min).

gameMission.title: The Island Train Timetable
gameMission.brief: Trains leave Measurement Island on the dot! Read each platform clock, compute how long passengers must wait, and catch Ravi announcing '70-minute hours' before the whole timetable melts.

realLifeProject: Be the family timekeeper for one evening: note the clock time when dinner starts and ends, when your favourite show starts and ends, and report each elapsed time by counting up.

rememberIt.hook: 60 seconds = 1 minute, 60 minutes = 1 hour, 24 hours = 1 day
rememberIt.unpack: Analog: count minutes in 5s. 24-hour: after noon add 12 (2 pm = 14:00). Elapsed time: count on.

studentQuestions.q: How do I convert 2 pm to 24-hour?
studentQuestions.a: Add 12 to afternoon times: 2 pm = 14:00. Morning stays the same (9 am = 09:00).

studentQuestions.q: How do I find elapsed time?
studentQuestions.a: Count on from start to end, hours then minutes - 3:40 to 5:10 is 1 hour 30 minutes.
